prompt:
Ticket: in-memory job orchestrator for the media-processing worker.

Today every worker binary hand-rolls its own goroutine soup for "run these jobs, respect their dependencies, don't melt the box", and we've been bitten by all of them. I want one library, package jobflow (module go-jobflow, repo root), that owns this. Split it into a few focused files — types/validation, graph checks, the scheduler, status bookkeeping — whatever cut you like, just not one monolith.

API the tests compile against: JobID and State string types with constants StatePending/StateRunning/StateSucceeded/StateFailed/StateSkipped/StateCancelled (values "pending", "running", "succeeded", "failed", "skipped", "cancelled"); JobSpec{ID JobID; Deps []JobID; Priority int; Run func(context.Context) error}; Options{MaxParallel int}; New(Options) (*Orchestrator, error); (*Orchestrator).Add(JobSpec) error; (*Orchestrator).Run(ctx) (map[JobID]State, error); (*Orchestrator).Status(JobID) (State, error).

Rules of the road: New rejects MaxParallel < 1. Add rejects empty IDs, nil Run funcs and duplicates (name the job in the error) and is refused once Run has been called; deps may point at jobs added later. Run is single-use, validates the whole graph up front (unknown dep errors name the dep, cycle errors contain the word "cycle") and then executes EVERY added job: a job starts only after all its deps succeeded, at most MaxParallel jobs run at once, and when a slot frees the highest-Priority ready job takes it (ties by Add order; jobs released by the same completion are weighed together). If a job errors it's failed, its transitive dependents are skipped and never invoked, unrelated branches keep going, and Run's error wraps the job's error (errors.Is-visible) and names it. Cancellation is the part I care most about, because it's where the old code always raced: every job gets a context derived from Run's; the moment that context is cancelled NO further job may be picked up — check the context at every dispatch decision, don't rely on a select branch winning — in-flight jobs are waited for (whatever they return decides succeeded/failed), and every job that never started ends up cancelled, taking precedence over would-be skip marking; Run then returns the final map plus an error satisfying errors.Is(err, context.Canceled). The returned map always covers every added job, even on failure or cancel. Status answers at any moment — before Run (pending), while things execute (running), after (terminal states) — so it must be safe under concurrent use; unknown IDs are an error.

jobflow_test.go is the acceptance contract — read it before designing, don't edit it. Green means `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` and it has to pass every time, not most times: the suite gates everything on channels and will call out both serialized execution and over-parallel execution.
